message: Investigated web_read issue: root cause is pi-search-hub web_read defaulting to anonymous Jina Reader; direct r.jina.ai and s.jina.ai calls return 401 AuthenticationRequiredError for AS7922. SAF is to configure a credentialed/verified reader at Pi search extension layer, not patch Subagent007.

Implemented no-secret web_read SAF in local Pi runtime: upgraded pi-search-hub to 2.8.0, set global reader to firecrawl, clarified web_read reader guidance, verified Subagent007 web_read returns Firecrawl results for example.com and npm registry.

Implemented three SAF repairs: parent-exit/process-group cleanup for fake child orphans; provider usage-limit reset/retry metadata propagation into results and failure logs; opt-in local active-child launch fuse with preflight rejection and no queue. Verified with typecheck, docs check, targeted tests, full npm test.

Implementation-tightener pass: removed unused active-child lease enabled flag and closed a promoted-run lease release gap if auto-promotion status persistence fails before the async child run is installed. Rebuilt and verified with typecheck, focused SAF tests, and full npm test.

Ran implementation-tightener pass over current child-capacity/usage-limit/process-cleanup diff. Typecheck passed; focused tests passed for usage-limit metadata, local child fuse, heartbeat cleanup, and parent-exit child-group cleanup. No behavior-preserving tightening applied.

Ran simplifier loop on current dirty diff. Reset .git/simplifier state, recorded npm test green baseline (191 pass), recorded scan map and two zero-admissible scans, generated .git/simplifier/REPORT.md. No findings or patches; finalize blocked only because worktree remains dirty/user-owned.

Simplifier loop finalized for SAF repairs: one low-risk lease-release duplication candidate was validated, full npm test passed 191/191, remaining capacity/cleanup/provider metadata code is load-bearing boundary behavior.

Cococo-cleaner pass reconciled local project-memory scaffold with source reality: documented package scripts/setup, removed broken pattern-index examples, and populated architecture/stack/conventions/decisions/router with verified Subagent007 Pi facts. Source behavior unchanged.
README clarified durable-run operation tools and local validation gates after verifying docs:check remains green.
README-fix documented provider usage-limit reset/retry metadata and primary source boundaries for agent readers. Verified with docs:check, diff check, and mex check.

Observed customer campaign SAF repaired public semantic failure projection: run-operation ValidationErrors now return operation_rejected instead of MCP text errors, required packet failures distinguish not-ready from invalid, and observed campaign oracles enforce the typed results. Verified with typecheck, focused tests, repaired full-current campaign, runtime readiness, docs:check, and npm test 191/191.

Implementation-tightener pass narrowed the observed probe result class from generic packet_failed to packet_required_not_ready so campaign coverage asserts the exact packet taxonomy repair instead of a loose packet-failure bucket. Verified with observed-campaign test, typecheck, docs:check, diff check, and mex check.

Simplifier SIM-001 removed operationRejectedResult's unreachable fallback reason-code computation; withRunFailureLogging remains the single reason-code computation point for operation rejection logging and structured MCP results. Static, targeted, and full source oracles passed.
Public prompt text is now projected to a redacted marker in public run views/transcript provenance, and observed campaigns assert prompt sentinels are absent from public artifacts, not only ledgers.
Tightened observed tool discovery coverage so campaigns assert required public tools and skill alias guidance instead of generic listTools success.
Observed tool-listing coverage now treats unexpected public MCP tools as a caller-visible defect, so discovery proves the exact public surface rather than only required-tool presence.

Public model calibration is class-only: MCP results, failure logs, session artifacts, README, and observed-campaign summaries must not expose concrete model IDs or thinking levels; observed probes now assert forbidden calibration fields are absent.
